{{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080724012153/http://www.yesterdayshero.com.au/PlayerProfile_Matt-Adamson_11262.aspx |date=24 July 2008}} |retired = yes |updated = 5 February 2021 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Phil Adamson]] (brother) }} }} '''Matt Adamson''' (born 14 August 1972 in [[Taree, New South Wales|Taree]], [[New South Wales]]) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A [[New South Wales Rugby League team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] and [[Australian Kangaroos|Australian]] international representative {{rlp|sr}} (under the [[Super League (Australia)|Super League]] banner), he played club football in Australia with the [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Penrith Panthers]] and [[Canberra Raiders]], and in England with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. Adamson started his career as a {{rlp|fb}} or utility, and was moved into the forwards by Penrith coach [[Royce Simmons]], in 1996. ==Career== A {{convert|6|ft|5|in|cm|adj=on}} tall fullback or winger in the early years of his career, Adamson was reportedly the second tallest fullback in top level rugby league history in Australia, second only to former [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]], [[Queensland Maroons|Queensland]], and Australian fullback [[Paul Hauff]] who stands {{convert|6|ft|6|in|cm|abbr=on}}. Adamson was later joined as the second tallest fullback in history by now former [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] and [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] utility back (he primarily played fullback for Souths), [[Greg Inglis]] who is also 6'5" tall. Adamson, who had signed with Super League in 1995 along with the rest of the Penrith Panthers and the club itself, made his test début from the bench for Australia in the [[1997 ANZAC Test|inaugural ANZAC Test]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in 1997, won 34–22 by Australia at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]]. He was then selected in the second row for the end of season test against the Kiwis at the [[North Harbour Stadium]] in [[Auckland]], won 30–12 by the Kiwis. Following the test in Auckland, Adamson was selected to play for Australia in all three matches of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Great Britain Lions|Great Britain]] in England. He was selected in the second row for the first test at London's famous [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]] which saw Australia win 38–14. He kept his place in the side for the second test at [[Old Trafford]], won 20–12 by the Lions, and was relegated to the bench for the deciding test at [[Elland Road]] in [[Leeds]] as the Australian's wrapped up the series with a 37–20 win. While the games are regarded as full test matches by the [[Rugby League International Federation]], the [[Australian Rugby League]] refuse to acknowledge the tests played under the Super League due to the [[Super League war]], and in their official records the games are not recorded. Adamson made his State of Origin début from the bench for NSW in Game 2 of the [[2001 State of Origin series]] at the [[ANZ Stadium|Telstra Stadium]] in Sydney. He was again selected from the bench for the third and deciding game of the series at [[Brisbane]]'s [[Suncorp Stadium]] in what would be his final representative game. After 10 seasons playing in Australia with Parramatta (1991) and Penrith (1993–2001), Adamson signed to play with English [[Super League]] team [[Leeds Rhinos]] following the [[2001 NRL season]]. He would spend three seasons with Leeds before returning to Australia to play for the Canberra Raiders in [[2005 NRL season|2005]]. Matt Adamson retired from playing at the end of the 2005 season. He played one game for Parramatta, 157 games for Penrith, 62 for Leeds and 16 for Canberra. ==Post Playing== Adamson worked as an assistant coach to [[Matthew Elliott (rugby league)|Matthew Elliott]] at [[Penrith Panthers]] during 2007 and 2008 but was sacked at the end of the 2008 season as part of a coaching "restructure."{{cite news|publisher=ninemsn|title=Adamson sacked in Penrith restructure|url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=634233|accessdate=20 September 2008|date=19 September 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080919161059/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=634233|archive-date=19 September 2008|url-status=dead}} Matt Adamson has worked a commentator for [[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]] in their telecasts of the [[National Rugby League]]. Appointed as the [[Melbourne Storm]] under 20's coach for the [[2014 Melbourne Storm season|2014 season]]. In 2015, he was appointed by the [[Melbourne Storm]] as their Queensland Coaching Director. The role saw him overseeing both feeder clubs [[Easts Tigers]] & [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]]. Matt coach the [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] national side for one game v [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] on 23 June 2018 which Fiji lost 26–14 in [[Campbelltown Stadium]], NSW.{{cite web|website=Rugby League Project|date=23 Jun 2018|accessdate=5 Feb 2021|title=PNG 26-14 Fiji|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/dates/23-june-2018/papua-new-guinea-vs-fiji.html}} ==Career highlights== * ''First-grade début:'' 26 July 1991 – Round 18 Parramatta Eels vs [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] at [[Parramatta Stadium]] ==Footnotes== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/playerstats/playerprofile/tabid/10898/clubid/2/playerid/2/seasonid/1/default.aspx NRL profile] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20081116235554/http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/pastplayers/squad-page.asp?ID=122 Profile at leedsrugby] {{S-start}} {{S-sport}} {{succession box | before = [[Mick Potter]]
